sanskrit:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते॥
transliteration:
anudvega-karaṃ vākyaṃ satyaṃ priya-hitaṃ ca yat |
svādhyāyābhyasanaṃ caiva vāṅmayaṃ tapa ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Speech that does not disturb, is true, dear, beneficial, and the regular practice of Vedic study—this is declared as verbal austerity.
contextual_meaning: Healthy speech is non-triggering, fact-based, relationally warm, and growth-oriented; it also includes disciplined study that refines the mind.
psychological_state: Mindful self-regulation of verbal output to reduce relational stress and enhance learning.
trigger_scenario: You’re about to tweet a snarky reply; instead you pause, rephrase with facts and kindness, then add a link to a source you’re studying.
anchor_text: Speak gently, truthfully, kindly, helpfully, and keep learning.
